question_text: A student constructs two circuits: Circuit A contains three identical resistors connected in series, whilst Circuit B contains the same three resistors connected in parallel, both powered by identical power supplies. Which statement correctly compares the total resistance in both circuits?

options.label: A
options.text: Circuit A has greater total resistance because resistances add in series

options.label: B
options.text: Circuit B has greater total resistance because more paths allow more resistance to accumulate

options.label: C
options.text: Both circuits have equal total resistance regardless of configuration

options.label: D
options.text: Circuit A has lower total resistance because components share the voltage equally

correct_answer: Circuit A has greater total resistance because resistances add in series
explanation: In a series circuit, resistances add together: R_total = R₁ + R₂ + R₃. In a parallel circuit, the total resistance is lower because current can take multiple paths. For identical resistors in parallel, the total resistance is less than any single resistor. Therefore, Circuit A (series) always has greater total resistance than Circuit B (parallel).
